div.p: Message: Invalid argument supplied for foreach()

verStmt.version: v1.1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes:
Version1:
Weights in General Household Survey 2016 v1 are based on figures provided by the 2013 mid-year population estimation model that incorporates the demographic findings of Census 2012. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
Person file contains 2 duplicate persons - uqnr: they are person number 7 from house “361101460000026201” and person 06 from house “593101670000005401”.
Version 1.1:
GHS 2016 version 1.1 includes revised weights. This version was released at the same time as GHS 2017 (21 June 2018). It was decided to replace the 2013 series mid-year population estimation in the previous version with a the more recent 2017 series mid-year population estimation as benchmarks for weighting the GHS data files. Household files were weighted independently of person files.
Apart from reweighting, the following changes were made by StatsSA in v1.1:
1. Q11Relsh - relationship to head; addressed cases in which persons aged < 10 were identified as parents to the head 2. Q16dfees - Added 0 option, which was excluded previously 3. Q28aProv - Gauteng and North West were swopped in the questionnaire and data file. Swopped back. Data not affected, just corrected the order. 4. Q1.3cFAR - father part of household: reviewed based on whether father was part of the household 5. Q13dFPSN - father person number: reviewed by verifying that personno referred to a male 6. Q14dMPSN - mother's person number: reviewed by verifying that personno referred to a female 7. Language spoken inside and outside the household added (this was asked, but not reported previously) 8. Added new weights - the weights were recalibrated using the 2017 series mid year population estimates 9. Two duplicates were removed.

stdyInfo.abstract: The GHS is an annual household survey which measures the living circumstances of South African households. The GHS collects data on education, health, and social development, housing, access to services and facilities, food security, and agriculture.

sumDscr.geogCover: The General Household Survey has national coverage.
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation for the data is Province (and metropolitan municipality, where this applies)
sumDscr.universe: The survey covers all de jure household members (usual residents) of households in the nine provinces of South Africa, and residents in workers' hostels. The survey does not cover collective living quarters such as student hostels, old age homes, hospitals, prisons, and military barracks.

stdyInfo.notes:
The scope of the General Household Survey includes:
Household characteristics: Dwelling type, home ownership, access to water and sanitation, access to services, transport, household assets, land ownership, agricultural production Individuals' characteristics: demographic characteristics, relationship to household head, marital status, language, education, employment, income, health, fertility, mortality, disability, access to social services

dataColl.sampProc: The sample design for the GHS was based on a master sample (MS) that was originally designed for the Quarterly Labour Force Survey (QLFS) and was used for the first time for the GHS in 2008. This master sample is shared by the QLFS, GHS, Living Conditions Survey (LCS), Domestic Tourism Survey (DTS) and the Income and Expenditure Survey (IES).
dataColl.resInstru: Data was collected with a household questionnaire and a questionnaire administered to a household member to elicit information on household members.
dataColl.weight:
The sample weights were constructed in order to account for the following: the original selection probabilities (design weights), adjustments for PSUs that were sub-sampled or segmented, excluded population from the sampling frame, non-response, weight trimming, and benchmarking to known population estimates from the Demographic Analysis Division within Stats SA.
The sampling weights for the data collected from the sampled households were constructed so that the responses could be properly expanded to represent the entire civilian population of South Africa. The design weights, which are the inverse sampling rate (ISR) for the province, are assigned to each of the households in a province.
Mid-year population estimates produced by the Demographic Analysis Division were used for benchmarking. The final survey weights were constructed using regression estimation to calibrate to national level population estimates cross-classified by 5-year age groups, gender and race, and provincial population estimates by broad age groups. The 5-year age groups are: 0–4, 5–9, 10–14, 55–59, 60–64; and 65 and over. The provincial level age groups are 0–14, 15–34, 35–64; and 65 years and over. The calibrated weights were constructed such that all persons in a household would have the same final weight.
The Statistics Canada software StatMx was used for constructing calibration weights. The population controls at national and provincial level were used for the cells defined by cross-classification of Age by Gender by Race. Records for which the age, population group or sex had item non-response could not be weighted and were therefore excluded from the dataset. No additional imputation was done to retain these records.
Household estimates that were developed using the UN headship ratio methodology were used to weight household files. The databases of Census 1996, Census 2001, Community Survey 2007 Census 2011 were used to analyse trends and develop models to predict the number of households for each year. The weighting system was based on tables for the expected distribution of household heads for specific age categories, per population group and province.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. General Household Survey 2016 [dataset]. Version 1.1. Pretoria: Statistics SA [producer], 2017. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2017. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/ka3w-rg96
useStmt.conditions: Public access data, available to all

catgry.labl: Dwelling/house or brick/concrete block structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling servants' quarters/granny flat
Dwelling/house or brick/concrete block structure on a separate stand or yard or on farm
Traditional dwelling/hut/structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Informal dwelling/shack not in backyard, e.g. in an informal/squatter settlement or on farm
Room/flatlet on a property or a larger dwelling servants' quarters/granny flat

var.name: Q822per
var.labl: person who answered most of the questions in section 5-8
var.imputation: person who answered most of the questions in section 5-8
var.security: person who answered most of the questions in section 5-8
var.embargo: person who answered most of the questions in section 5-8
var.respUnit: person who answered most of the questions in section 5-8

var.name: Q92Membersdied
var.labl: Members of the household who passed away
var.imputation: Members of the household who passed away
var.security: Members of the household who passed away
var.embargo: Members of the household who passed away
var.respUnit: Members of the household who passed away

catgry.labl: Other relative (e.g. in-laws or aunt/uncle) of person 01
Living together like husband and wife
Single, but have been living together with someone as husband and/wife before
Single and have never been married/never lived together as husband/wife before
Post Higher Diploma (Technikon/University of Technology Masters, Doctoral)

var.name: Q16ATECHILD
var.labl: Type of ECD facility children attend
var.imputation: Type of ECD facility children attend
var.security: Type of ECD facility children attend
var.embargo: Type of ECD facility children attend
var.respUnit: Type of ECD facility children attend

var.name: Q16BNOTATTE
var.labl: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.imputation: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.security: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.embargo: Place where children spent most of their time during the day
var.respUnit: Place where children spent most of their time during the day

catgry.labl: At home with parent, foster parent or guardian
At home with someone younger than 18 years

var.name: Q16CMAINREASN
var.labl: Main reason for not attending the ECD facility
var.imputation: Main reason for not attending the ECD facility
var.security: Main reason for not attending the ECD facility
var.embargo: Main reason for not attending the ECD facility
var.respUnit: Main reason for not attending the ECD facility

catgry.labl: Prefer that the child stay at home/with someone else
These facilities do not exist in our area

var.name: Q16DFEES
var.labl: Amount paid to attend the ECD facility
var.imputation: Amount paid to attend the ECD facility
var.security: Amount paid to attend the ECD facility
var.embargo: Amount paid to attend the ECD facility
var.respUnit: Amount paid to attend the ECD facility

var.name: Q17ATAKCARE
var.labl: Number of the main person who take care of the child
var.imputation: Number of the main person who take care of the child
var.security: Number of the main person who take care of the child
var.embargo: Number of the main person who take care of the child
var.respUnit: Number of the main person who take care of the child

var.name: Q17BRELATIONSHP
var.labl: Relationship of the main person who take care of the child
var.imputation: Relationship of the main person who take care of the child
var.security: Relationship of the main person who take care of the child
var.embargo: Relationship of the main person who take care of the child
var.respUnit: Relationship of the main person who take care of the child

var.name: Q18BOBJECT
var.labl: Ask/explain what the object is used for
var.imputation: Ask/explain what the object is used for
var.security: Ask/explain what the object is used for
var.embargo: Ask/explain what the object is used for
var.respUnit: Ask/explain what the object is used for

catgry.labl: At home and sent to facility with the child
Prepared elsewhere and delivered to the facility
Has completed education/satisfied with my level of education/do not want to study
He or she is working at home or business/job
Education is useless or not interesting
Pre-school (including ECD centre, e.g. day care, crèche, play group, nursery school or pre-primary school)
School (including Grade R to Grade 12 learners who attended a formal
Adult Basic Education and Training Learning Centre (AET Centre)
Other than any of the above
Minibus/bus provided by institution/government and not paid for
Vehicle hired by a group of parents
Own car or other private vehicle

var.name: Q115bTIM
var.labl: Time taken to travel to school
var.imputation: Time taken to travel to school
var.security: Time taken to travel to school
var.embargo: Time taken to travel to school
var.respUnit: Time taken to travel to school

var.name: Q115dNNI
var.labl: Reason for not attending the nearest institution
var.imputation: Reason for not attending the nearest institution
var.security: Reason for not attending the nearest institution
var.embargo: Reason for not attending the nearest institution
var.respUnit: Reason for not attending the nearest institution

var.name: Q116FEET
var.labl: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.imputation: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.security: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.embargo: Total amount of tuition fees paid
var.respUnit: Total amount of tuition fees paid

catgry.labl: School did not ask for fees (no-fee school)
Got a bursary covering all costs

var.name: Q119cTABS
var.labl: Teachers are often absent from school
var.imputation: Teachers are often absent from school
var.security: Teachers are often absent from school
var.embargo: Teachers are often absent from school
var.respUnit: Teachers are often absent from school

var.name: Q119cPMAN
var.labl: Teachers were involved in a strike action
var.imputation: Teachers were involved in a strike action
var.security: Teachers were involved in a strike action
var.embargo: Teachers were involved in a strike action
var.respUnit: Teachers were involved in a strike action

catgry.labl: Yes, a few times a week

var.name: Q123aVIOL
var.labl: Violence corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.imputation: Violence corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.security: Violence corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.embargo: Violence corporal punishment or verbal abuse
var.respUnit: Violence corporal punishment or verbal abuse

catgry.labl: Not applicable - school closed e.g. school holiday
Did not want to go to school
Need to take care of someone else at home
Does not feel safe at school

var.name: Q23FLU
var.labl: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.imputation: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.security: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.embargo: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection
var.respUnit: Flu or acute respiratory tract infection

var.name: Q23SCblood
var.labl: TB or severe cough with blood
var.imputation: TB or severe cough with blood
var.security: TB or severe cough with blood
var.embargo: TB or severe cough with blood
var.respUnit: TB or severe cough with blood

catgry.labl: Not necessary/the problem was not serious enough
Experienced difficulty getting a diagnosis before

var.name: Q25aSTR
var.labl: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating
var.imputation: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating
var.security: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating
var.embargo: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating
var.respUnit: Severe trauma due to violence, assault, beating

catgry.labl: Does not go to school or work
The child has been born alive
The child died in the womb or during childbirth after the 7th month of pregnancy (stillbirth)
The child died in the womb or the pregnancy ended before the 7th month of pregnancy (spontaneous abortion/miscarriage)
The pregnancy was ended by choice before the child was born (termination of pregnancy/abortion by choice)

var.name: Q28ERECIEVDATE
var.labl: Receive date of the birth certificate
var.imputation: Receive date of the birth certificate
var.security: Receive date of the birth certificate
var.embargo: Receive date of the birth certificate
var.respUnit: Receive date of the birth certificate

catgry.labl: Within 30 days (1month) after the child's birth
Between 30 and 90 days (1 month-3 months after birth)
More than 90 days (3 months after the child's birth)

var.name: Q41aWGE
var.labl: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.imputation: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.security: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.embargo: Working for a wage, commission or salary
var.respUnit: Working for a wage, commission or salary

catgry.labl: Lift club by a group of people sharing a private vehicle

var.name: Q44bMIN
var.labl: Time taken to place of employment
var.imputation: Time taken to place of employment
var.security: Time taken to place of employment
var.embargo: Time taken to place of employment
var.respUnit: Time taken to place of employment

catgry.labl: In the formal sector (registered to perform activity)
In the informal sector (not registered to perform activity)

var.name: Q46aLOOK
var.labl: Looking for any kind of job or trying to start a business
var.imputation: Looking for any kind of job or trying to start a business
var.security: Looking for any kind of job or trying to start a business
var.embargo: Looking for any kind of job or trying to start a business
var.respUnit: Looking for any kind of job or trying to start a business

var.name: Q46cRNSW
var.labl: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.imputation: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.security: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.embargo: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business
var.respUnit: Reason for not trying to find work or starting a business

catgry.labl: Waiting to be recalled to former job
Undergoing training to help find work
No jobs available in the area
Lack of money to pay for transport to look for work
Unable to find work requiring his/her skills
Lost hope of finding any kind of work

var.name: Q47JCR
var.labl: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme
var.imputation: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme
var.security: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme
var.embargo: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme
var.respUnit: Participated in Government or municipal job creation programme
